data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

This comment reviews the FOLLOW-ON plan "Reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry (coverage.metrics) on the workflow plan-review gate" (re-add det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls that B-RETIRE dropped with run_review). T11 is an overlay (migration-EXECUTION safety) that fires only when a plan changes a persisted schema/format IN-PLACE or BACKFILLS existing data. This plan does neither.

Grounded reasoning:

- ADDITIVE, FORWARD-ONLY CAPTURE: All three ACs are about EMITTING `coverage['metrics']` going forward — the workflow path captures step timings during the run and carries them on the (already-existing, additive) `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar event. The sidecar is an append-only event in rebar's file-per-event git log (sidecar.py:36-54 emit → append_event), not a relational table. No existing event/record format is rewritten in place; T11(a) expand-contract / add-nullable→backfill→enforce / blocking-DDL has no surface.

- NO BACKFILL OF HISTORICAL DATA: The plan re-instruments the live workflow run to PRODUCE metrics on NEW reviews; it does not read prior REVIEW_RESULT events and repopulate them. Pre-existing sidecars emitted during the regression window simply lack `metrics` and are left untouched — tolerated because `coverage` is a free-form object (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json` coverage `additionalProperties: true`). So T11(b) batching/throttling, (c) resumability/idempotent re-run, (d) dual-write window, and (e) partial-failure rollback have nothing to apply to.

- NO BLOCKING DDL / NO LARGE-TABLE LOCK: append-only git-backed event store, not DDL on a large table. The T11 MAJOR trigger (irreversible single-shot migration / long blocking lock on a large table) has no surface here.

- The change is to in-memory dict assembly (`coverage['metrics']`) flowing into the sidecar payload — confirmed there is no current producer (`search_files "coverage[\"metrics\"]"` over src/rebar/llm/plan_review returns no matches), consistent with the described regression; the plan adds the producer forward-only.

ANTI-FP: T11 is explicitly not-applicable for additive/non-rewriting persistence changes with no backfill of existing data.

Affirmations (T11): The plan's own text affirmatively establishes a purely additive, forward-capture-only instrumentation change (emit metrics on the workflow path; carry them on the existing append-only sidecar; test that they are produced) with no persisted-format rewrite, no blocking DDL, and no backfill of historical sidecars. Under the overlay gate + ANTI-FP clause, T11 is not-applicable — clean, no findings.
